summary: Review of changes addressing greptile-apps[bot] review feedback: (1) hardcoded keystone-manage replaced with dynamic ${MATRIX_SERVICE}-manage, (2) smoke-test job restructured with its own matrix strategy and independent image-ref derivation to eliminate single-output limitation, (3) wc -l output trimmed with xargs. All 60 verification tests pass. YAML validates. One documentation accuracy issue found: smoke-test permissions section in docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md omits contents: read.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases covered: dynamic smoke test command, matrix strategy, independent tag derivation
New tests for smoke-test matrix (test_smoke_test_has_matrix) and independent derivation (test_smoke_test_derives_image_ref)

code_quality_checklist.item: Expression injection defense via env vars consistently applied
Tag derivation logic duplicated between build-service-images and smoke-test (acceptable GHA limitation)

security_checklist.item: No direct interpolation of matrix values in run: blocks (expression injection defense)
GITHUB_TOKEN used for GHCR auth (no hardcoded secrets)
Job-level permissions scoped appropriately (packages: write only on build jobs)

architecture_checklist.item: smoke-test job independently derives image refs via own matrix (eliminates single-output limitation)
Dynamic service-manage dispatch works for any OpenStack service added to matrix

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Tag derivation duplication is justified by GHA job isolation (no shared shell between jobs)
Matrix must be kept in sync between build-service-images and smoke-test (documented in Adding a New Service section)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Smoke test fails workflow on non-zero exit from service-manage --version
wc -l whitespace trimmed with xargs to prevent invalid Docker tags

defensive_checklist.item: PATCH_COUNT defaults to 0 when patches directory absent
Branch name sanitized (slash to dash) for Docker tag compatibility

issues_found.description: Documentation permissions section for smoke-test job is inaccurate after adding checkout step. The docs show only `packages: read` but the code has both `contents: read` and `packages: read`. In GHA, job-level permissions OVERRIDE (not merge with) top-level permissions, so `contents: read` is required for the checkout step to work. The text at line 55-56 stating 'The smoke-test job receives only packages: read' is wrong.
issues_found.fix: Update the smoke-test permissions snippet (line 48-51) to include `contents: read` alongside `packages: read`. Update the explanatory text (line 55-56) to mention both permissions: 'The smoke-test job receives contents: read (for checkout to access source-refs.yaml) and packages: read (sufficient for pulling images), following the principle of least privilege.'

suggested_improvements: D3/Q7: The build-service-images output `image-ref` (line 82) is no longer consumed by any job within this workflow since smoke-test now derives its own refs. The NOTE comment explains the limitation but consider removing the output entirely to avoid confusion, or documenting it as available for external workflow_call consumers.
next_steps: Fix DA3-1: Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md smoke-test permissions section to include contents: read

metadata.title: C002: Add container image CI workflow

external_review_fixes_verified.hardcoded_keystone_manage: Fixed: dynamic ${MATRIX_SERVICE}-manage in both PR inline and push smoke-test
external_review_fixes_verified.matrix_output_limitation: Fixed: smoke-test has own matrix + independent tag derivation
external_review_fixes_verified.wc_whitespace: Fixed: | xargs in both build-service-images and smoke-test
